puzzleDescription: Characters in Java have numeric values (ASCII). Learn how to multiply a float by a character's value and handle type precision.
objective: 1. Declare a char that has any letter value. 2. Declare a float set a value of 2.5f. 3. Calculate the product of the character's ASCII value and the float value. 4. Print the result.
hints: In Java, 'char' can be used in math equations; it uses its ASCII value (A=65, B=66, etc.).
Floats MUST have an 'f' at the end (e.g., 2.5f). Without it, Java thinks it is a double.
When you multiply a char by a float, the result becomes a float.
